review_title: super unproductive workplace with toxic team leads
review_body_text: they will gaslight you, throw you under the bus & use you as scapegoat for their mistakes. I learn how to survive in this harsh environment. fresh joiners keep quitting cause of the treatment they get as the job scope on paper is not as what you are doing at work. alot of glorified admin work to do for your team leads
The colleauges are friendly and the culture is good. Team work is showing here. The downsides are slow career progression and limited training is provided.
Good staff benefit, good leadership. A place to grow and develop your skills. Friendly environment and colleagues work well with each other. Would recommend friends to join the bank
People at there are friendly and the working environment is good. So is it friendly for fresh graduate student who want to try to step into banking industry
No worklife balance. No racism issues. However, slow progression. The job was fun, but it is quite boring as days goes by. If you're an active person, I do not recommend.

review_title: Average pay, bad management, good staff benefits
review_body_text: Very stressful environment with boss yelling, fast paced environment. But the working hours are from 8.30am-5.30pm only and if got OT, it is until 7.30pm. Still consider early than other banks/dept that require longer working hours until 9-10pm. However, it’s advisable to stay back to cover up the kpi.
I got to improve my skill and learn how things run. I improve in communication and learn to be accountable to my department. Improve my coding abilities as well

review_title: Company culture might not suit for certain people
review_body_text: Pros: Benefit and competitive salary. Cons: Toxic environment and most employee are very lack of skills and experience with no motivation for improvement which make very hard to work as a team.
Got flexi hours, flex benefits, good relationship with staff, got celebrations such as hari raya, cny, deepavali, merry christmast and etc. Career Growth and Wise salary.
you will find wonderful people here. It is people culture. Financial system is well established over the years and the management always emphasized on sustainability
cons_text: voices from lower base may not be heard due to high activity of the management

review_body_text: Fast paced, training less than a month. But bad management and environment as well. -Culture not so bad, but they will expect you to be more productive. -Workplace not fun -short break time, not even give 30 minutes one day, sometimes they ask you why you take long break. -Many staff resign because with their management
cons_text: No worklife balance, stressful with management.

review_body_text: good learning experience....meet a lot of people.....improve in communication skill...have to work overtime and be carefully in processing the telegraphic transfer

review_title: Depends on what location & role you are
review_body_text: I can say I kind of enjoyed when working at ocbc. They provided full training for all the positions, and you are allowed to internally transfer to other roles if you wanted to. You can apply for scholarships if you wanted to further study (but must be related). It's a great start if you are from a small company/industry outside, ocbc benefits are much better than theirs & annual leave is at least 19 days if not mistaken.
pros_text: Medical claims, allowance provided based on position, flex claim (can claim anything)
cons_text: Stress for sales role, lack of management

review_body_text: Good company with lots of benefits, but high target high staff turnover, stressful environment, one bank but many departments inside, each department in charge of their own stuff, don’t expect people from different department will assist you, limited products for sales.

review_title: High turn over rate especially vendors
review_body_text: Good to gain experience. Job scope is very straightforward although it is fast paced. You will feel different treatment as a vendor against the staff at the client site. You will be forced to stay late by management because of their dinasour mindset where staying late is equivalent to being committed. Management is so desperate they will hire anyone and put the training responsibilities to seniors when they’re already short staffed. They will smooth talk to you by saying you will have increment and promotion. But at the end you will get peanuts.
pros_text: You can learn soft skills over the time
cons_text: No compensation given. No OT. Small amount of medical allowance.

review_body_text: Management were horrible. Dont work here if u want to find a long career life. Trust me if u jump to this company, within one month u will started to find a new job and the turnover at this company was too high. U can saw everyday there will be a staff that will resign

review_title: fun colleagues but of course every company have few rotten tomatoes
review_body_text: multi-tasking is a must friendly working environment the hardest part is long working hours and high volume especially after US holiday. limited career growth
Senior colleague are friendly and is willing to guide you and is willing to provide feedback on personal performance. Good working environment and convenient.

review_title: If you get Best TL this is your place to stay forever
review_body_text: Good knowledgeable trainer place to success and career growth overall happy and satisfied Product knowledge arrange for your everything based on fingertips

review_title: Productive and good kickstart of career workplace to work at.
review_body_text: -Ambiance and good working surrounding. -Close to public transport LRT Kerinchi Station -Located at Menara TM, Bangsar Jalan Pantai Bharu -Fast-paced working environment but can treat as a new challenge especially for fresh grads -Offer beneficial remuneration compared to other bank
cons_text: Lack of empathy towards employee when have emergency like flood situation
